_sanitization: Public release of an internal research matrix. Vendor names in notes have been generalized to architecture categories. Internal tool flags, tool roadmap, and proprietary execution metadata have been removed. See PRIVATE_OMITTED.md in the source repo for the audit log.
_description: Attack effectiveness matrix: maps each vector against protection architectures and config levels. Effectiveness levels: deadly (service failure expected), degraded (partial impact, user-visible), mitigated (absorbed with minor impact), blocked (no impact). Config levels: default (vendor defaults, no tuning), tuned (basic policies configured), hardened (expert-level config, behavioral baselines trained).
_notes: This matrix captures effectiveness AGAINST the protection layer, not the origin behind it.
For hybrid architectures, effectiveness = min(CDN effectiveness, on-prem effectiveness) for each vector.
Cloud CDN/WAF 'typical' and 'max' refer to aggregate provider capacity, not per-customer limits.
Protocol-specific vectors (SIP, SMTP, SSH, etc.) only apply to assets running those services.
Amplification vectors use bots as reflectors - the amp factor multiplies bot bandwidth.
Prerequisites field lists conditions that must be true for the attack to work at stated effectiveness.

architectures.exposed: No CDN, WAF, or DDoS mitigation. Traffic hits origin directly.
architectures.cloud_cdn: Cloud CDN/WAF (Cloudflare, Akamai, Radware Cloud, Imperva, CloudFront). Elastic, distributed.
architectures.security_waf: On-prem security WAF (F5 BIG-IP, Check Point, Palo Alto, FortiGate). DPI shares CPU with IPS/AV/SSL.
architectures.ddos_appliance: Dedicated DDoS appliance (Radware DefensePro, Arbor, A10 Thunder TPS). Hardware-accelerated, behavioral.
architectures.hybrid: Cloud CDN + on-prem appliance. Two layers, CDN absorbs volumetric, appliance handles L7.
architectures.perimeter_filtered: Firewall/ACL filtering. Blocks unsolicited traffic but no DDoS-specific mitigation.

config_levels.default: Vendor defaults. No custom policies, no rate limits configured, no behavioral baselines trained.
config_levels.tuned: Basic policies: rate limits set, geo-blocking enabled, bot signatures updated, WAF rules active but not customized.
config_levels.hardened: Expert config: behavioral baselines trained (2+ weeks), custom rate limits per endpoint, challenge pages for suspicious patterns, origin lockdown, header stripping, TLS 1.3 only.

l3_icmp_flood.name: ICMP Flood
l3_icmp_flood.mechanism: Volumetric pipe saturation with ICMP echo packets. Targets ISP uplink bandwidth.

exposed.notes: Origin pipe saturates. Hardened: kernel ICMP rate limiting helps but pipe still fills.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy ICMP. Only works if attacker knows origin IP and bypasses CDN.

security_waf.notes: WAF drops ICMP but shares pipe with legitimate traffic. High volume still saturates uplink.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware ICMP drop at line rate. Only deadly if volume exceeds appliance rated capacity.

hybrid.notes: CDN absorbs at edge. ICMP never reaches on-prem.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops ICMP. Pipe saturation still possible with extreme volume.

l3_blacknurse.name: BlackNurse (ICMP Type 3)
l3_blacknurse.mechanism: ICMP Type 3 Code 3 (port unreachable) at moderate rate causes CPU spike on firewalls. Only ~40Kpps needed.

exposed.notes: Kernel processes each packet. Hardened: iptables rate limit on ICMP type 3.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't forward ICMP. Requires origin IP.

security_waf.notes: Firewall CPU processes ICMP unreachable. BlackNurse specifically targets firewall CPUs.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops anomalous ICMP patterns.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall processes ICMP - this is the exact target of BlackNurse.

l3_icmp_tunnel.name: ICMP Tunneling
l3_icmp_tunnel.mechanism: Encapsulate data in ICMP payloads. Used for C2 exfil, not volumetric DDoS.

exposed.notes: Low volume, not a DDoS vector per se. Resource drain from processing oversized ICMP.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy ICMP.

security_waf.notes: DPI inspects ICMP payloads - CPU cost. Tuned: drop oversized ICMP.

ddos_appliance.notes: Anomalous ICMP payload detection.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops ICMP or limits payload size.

l3_ip_fragment.name: IP Fragmentation Flood
l3_ip_fragment.mechanism: Flood with fragmented IP packets. Target must reassemble, consuming CPU and memory. Overlapping fragments cause additional processing.

exposed.notes: Kernel fragment reassembly exhausts memory. Hardened: sysctl fragment limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN edge drops fragments or reassembles with massive capacity.

security_waf.notes: WAF reassembly consumes CPU. Tuned: fragment reassembly limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware fragment reassembly at line rate.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall reassembly. Tuned: drop fragmented packets.

l3_ip_null.name: IP Null (Protocol 0)
l3_ip_null.mechanism: IP packets with protocol field 0 (HOPOPT). Confuses stateful inspection, may bypass ACLs.

exposed.notes: Kernel processes unknown protocol. Tuned: iptables drop non-TCP/UDP/ICMP.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN only proxies HTTP/HTTPS.

security_waf.notes: DPI attempts to classify. Tuned: explicit protocol whitelist.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops unknown protocols.

l3_gre_flood.name: GRE Tunnel Flood
l3_gre_flood.mechanism: Flood with GRE (protocol 47) packets. Targets tunnel endpoints and pipe bandwidth.

exposed.notes: Pipe saturation. Hardened: drop GRE if not used.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy GRE.

security_waf.notes: WAF processes GRE decap. Tuned: drop if no GRE tunnels configured.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware GRE rate limiting.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops GRE unless explicitly allowed.

l3_esp_flood.name: ESP Flood (IPSec)
l3_esp_flood.mechanism: Flood with ESP (protocol 50) packets. Targets IPSec VPN endpoints.

exposed.notes: If VPN endpoint: crypto processing exhaustion. If not: kernel drops but pipe fills.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy ESP.

security_waf.notes: Firewall processes ESP for VPN. Tuned: rate limit ESP, drop if no VPN.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops non-VPN ESP.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops ESP unless VPN configured.

l3_ah_flood.name: AH Flood (IPSec)
l3_ah_flood.mechanism: Flood with AH (protocol 51) packets. Similar to ESP but targets authentication header processing.

exposed.notes: Same as ESP flood.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy AH.

security_waf.notes: Same as ESP.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l3_ipip_flood.name: IP-in-IP Tunnel Flood
l3_ipip_flood.mechanism: Flood with IP-in-IP (protocol 4) encapsulated packets. Targets tunnel decapsulation.

exposed.notes: Kernel decap processing. Hardened: drop protocol 4 if unused.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy.

security_waf.notes: DPI on encapsulated packets. Tuned: drop if unused.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l3_ipv6ip_flood.name: IPv6-in-IPv4 Flood
l3_ipv6ip_flood.mechanism: IPv6 packets tunneled in IPv4 (protocol 41). Targets 6-to-4 gateways.

exposed.notes: Gateway processing. Hardened: drop protocol 41 if no 6to4.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy.

security_waf.notes: Firewall may not inspect inner IPv6. Tuned: drop.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l3_mpls_flood.name: MPLS Flood
l3_mpls_flood.mechanism: Flood with MPLS-labeled packets targeting label switch routers.

exposed.notes: Only effective against MPLS-enabled routers. Most servers drop silently.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't forward MPLS.

security_waf.notes: WAF drops non-IP.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l4_syn_flood.name: SYN Flood
l4_syn_flood.mechanism: Spoofed SYN packets exhaust TCP connection table (SYN backlog). Classic volumetric + state exhaustion.

exposed.notes: Connection table fills. Hardened: SYN cookies, increased backlog, but pipe still saturates.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates TCP. SYN flood never reaches origin.

security_waf.notes: SYN proxy/cookies on WAF. But WAF connection table is finite and shared.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware SYN proxy at line rate. Designed for this exact attack.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall SYN proxy. Limited connection table.

l4_synack_flood.name: SYN-ACK Flood (Reflection)
l4_synack_flood.mechanism: Spoofed SYN to reflectors causes SYN-ACK flood to victim. Bypasses ingress SYN filtering.

exposed.notes: Victim receives SYN-ACK for connections it never initiated. Confuses stateful firewalls.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops unsolicited SYN-ACK.

security_waf.notes: Stateful firewall confused by unsolicited SYN-ACK. Tuned: strict TCP state tracking.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection of SYN-ACK without matching SYN.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Stateful firewall should drop. May still consume CPU on state lookup.

l4_ack_flood.name: ACK Flood
l4_ack_flood.mechanism: Spoofed ACK packets bypass SYN proxy (already 'established'). Target wastes CPU on state lookup for non-existent connections.

exposed.notes: Kernel searches connection table for each ACK. High PPS = CPU exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops ACK without matching connection.

security_waf.notes: WAF state table lookup for each ACK. Tuned: strict state tracking drops faster.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware state tracking at line rate.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall state lookup. Tuned: drop ACK without SYN.

l4_fin_flood.name: FIN Flood
l4_fin_flood.mechanism: Spoofed FIN packets. Similar to ACK flood - forces state table lookup.

exposed.notes: Same as ACK flood mechanism.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops.

security_waf.notes: Same as ACK.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l4_rst_flood.name: RST Flood
l4_rst_flood.mechanism: Spoofed RST packets to tear down legitimate connections (if sequence number guessed) or exhaust state table processing.

exposed.notes: RST with valid seq# kills connections. Random RST still causes state lookup. Hardened: RST window validation.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops RST without matching state.

security_waf.notes: WAF processes RST for state cleanup. CPU cost.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware RST validation.

l4_pshack_flood.name: PSH+ACK Flood
l4_pshack_flood.mechanism: PSH+ACK with payload forces application layer processing. Bypasses SYN proxy since it looks like established connection data.

exposed.notes: Application processes each payload. Higher CPU cost than pure ACK flood.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops without matching connection state.

security_waf.notes: DPI engine processes payload of each packet. Very expensive for security WAFs.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware state validation drops.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops. May process payload for IPS matching first.

l4_tsunami_tcp.name: Tsunami TCP (SYN+Timestamp)
l4_tsunami_tcp.mechanism: SYN flood with timestamp options. Bypasses basic SYN cookies that don't encode timestamps.

exposed.notes: Same as SYN flood but bypasses naive SYN cookie implementations.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates TCP.

security_waf.notes: WAF SYN proxy may not handle timestamp options correctly.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware SYN proxy handles timestamps.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall SYN proxy may not handle.

l4_tcp_fragment.name: TCP Fragment Flood
l4_tcp_fragment.mechanism: TCP segments fragmented at IP layer. Forces reassembly before TCP processing.

exposed.notes: IP reassembly + TCP processing double cost. Hardened: drop fragments.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN handles reassembly at massive scale.

security_waf.notes: WAF must reassemble to inspect. Expensive. Tuned: fragment limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware reassembly at line rate.

l4_tcp_connection.name: TCP Connection Flood (Empty)
l4_tcp_connection.mechanism: Complete TCP handshake then hold connection open without sending data. Exhausts connection table with real connections (not spoofable).

exposed.notes: Server connection limit hit. Apache default: 256 workers. Hardened: aggressive timeouts.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN has massive connection capacity. But connection to origin may be limited.

security_waf.notes: WAF connection table shared with all functions. Exhausts faster than rated.

ddos_appliance.notes: Large connection tables (40M+) but still finite. Behavioral detection of idle connections.

hybrid.notes: CDN terminates and pools connections to origin.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall connection table. Tuned: idle timeout.

prerequisites: Cannot use spoofed IPs - requires real TCP handshake from bot IPs

l4_sockstress.name: Sockstress
l4_sockstress.mechanism: Complete handshake, set window to 0, force server to hold buffered data indefinitely. Memory exhaustion.

exposed.notes: Server buffers data for zero-window peers. Memory exhaustion. Hardened: socket buffer limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates TCP. May still affect origin if CDN forwards idle connections.

security_waf.notes: WAF buffers for zero-window clients. Shared memory with DPI engine.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects persistent zero-window. Tuned: timeout zero-window connections.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall may not inspect window size.

prerequisites: Requires real TCP connections from bot IPs

l4_custom_flags.name: Custom TCP Flags (XMAS/NULL)
l4_custom_flags.mechanism: Packets with unusual flag combinations (all flags=XMAS, no flags=NULL). Confuses stateful inspection.

exposed.notes: Kernel drops invalid flags quickly. Main use is scanning/evasion, not DDoS.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops invalid TCP.

security_waf.notes: DPI engine processes each packet to classify. Tuned: drop invalid flags immediately.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops invalid TCP at line rate.

l4_udp_flood.name: UDP Flood
l4_udp_flood.mechanism: Volumetric UDP flood. Spoofed source IPs. Targets pipe bandwidth and ICMP unreachable generation.

exposed.notes: Pipe saturation. Kernel generates ICMP unreachable per packet (CPU). Hardened: iptables rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN only proxies HTTP/HTTPS. UDP dropped at edge. Only works if origin IP known.

security_waf.notes: WAF drops UDP to HTTP ports but shares pipe with legitimate traffic.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware UDP rate limiting and behavioral detection.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall drops UDP. Pipe may still saturate.

l4_udp_fragment.name: UDP Fragmentation Flood
l4_udp_fragment.mechanism: Fragmented UDP packets. Combines IP reassembly cost with UDP flood volume.

exposed.notes: Same as IP fragment + UDP flood combined.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops.

security_waf.notes: Reassembly + inspection CPU. Very expensive for DPI.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware reassembly.

l4_dns_torture.name: DNS Water Torture
l4_dns_torture.mechanism: Random subdomain queries (rnd123.target.com) that cache cannot answer. Forces recursive resolver and authoritative DNS to process each query.

exposed.notes: Authoritative DNS exhausted. NXDOMAIN for each random query. Hardened: DNS rate limiting, response rate limiting (RRL).

cloud_cdn.notes: If DNS managed by CDN: rate limiting helps. If authoritative is separate: CDN doesn't protect DNS.

security_waf.notes: WAF typically doesn't inspect DNS. DNS server behind WAF still processes all queries.

ddos_appliance.notes: Some appliances have DNS-aware mitigation. DDoS appliance can detect water torture patterns.

hybrid.notes: CDN may protect DNS if managed. Otherwise DNS is separate attack surface.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall passes DNS through. No DNS-specific mitigation.

prerequisites: Target must have authoritative DNS servers

l4_isakmp_flood.name: ISAKMP Flood (IKE)
l4_isakmp_flood.mechanism: Flood IKE/ISAKMP (UDP/500) to exhaust VPN gateway crypto processing.

exposed.notes: VPN gateway processes each IKE init. Crypto CPU exhaustion. Only if VPN exposed.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy UDP/500.

security_waf.notes: Firewall with VPN: crypto processing on same CPU. Non-VPN: drops.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limiting IKE init.

prerequisites: Target must expose VPN endpoint on UDP/500

l4_quic_flood.name: QUIC Flood (UDP/443)
l4_quic_flood.mechanism: Flood QUIC Initial packets (UDP/443). Server must perform crypto handshake for each. High CPU cost.

exposed.notes: QUIC server processes crypto for each Initial. Hardened: retry token, connection migration limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN has massive QUIC capacity. Rate limiting QUIC handshakes.

security_waf.notes: WAF may not inspect QUIC (encrypted). Passes through to server.

ddos_appliance.notes: Newer appliances have QUIC awareness. Older ones pass through.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall passes UDP/443 if QUIC enabled. No QUIC-specific mitigation.

amp_ntp.name: NTP Amplification
amp_ntp.mechanism: Bot sends monlist query to open NTP servers with spoofed source=victim. Amplification factor ~556x.

exposed.notes: Massive volume. Even small bot fleet generates Tbps-level floods.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN only accepts HTTP/HTTPS. NTP UDP reflected to origin IP only works if origin exposed.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates before WAF can process. WAF is irrelevant at this volume.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance drops NTP at line rate but pipe upstream may saturate.

hybrid.notes: CDN absorbs. Origin not directly hit.

prerequisites: Uses bot fleet as reflectors (spoofed source IP)
Requires open NTP servers with monlist enabled

amp_dns.name: DNS Amplification
amp_dns.mechanism: Bot sends ANY query to open DNS resolvers with spoofed source=victim. Amplification ~28-54x.

exposed.notes: Volume overwhelms pipe. Hardened: upstream ISP scrubbing helps.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN only accepts HTTP. DNS reflection hits origin IP.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates. WAF irrelevant.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops DNS responses on non-DNS ports. Pipe may still saturate.

amp_sip.name: SIP Amplification
amp_sip.mechanism: SIP OPTIONS to open SIP servers. Amplification ~10x.

exposed.notes: Lower amp factor but still effective for pipe saturation.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN only accepts HTTP.

security_waf.notes: Lower volume than NTP/DNS amp. WAF may handle.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

exposed.notes: High volume. SSDP responses are large XML.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturation risk.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

amp_snmp.name: SNMP Amplification
amp_snmp.mechanism: GetBulk queries to SNMP agents. Amplification ~6x.

exposed.notes: Volume from many reflectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Lower amp factor.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

prerequisites: SNMP agents with public community string

exposed.notes: High amp factor. Massive volume.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops. Pipe may saturate upstream.

amp_rdp.name: RDP Amplification

exposed.notes: High amp. Volume floods pipe.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops RDP UDP.

prerequisites: Exposed RDP servers with UDP transport enabled

amp_memcached.name: Memcached Amplification
amp_memcached.mechanism: Memcached GET on pre-seeded keys. Amplification ~51000x. The most powerful known amplification vector.

exposed.notes: 51000x amplification. Single bot can generate multi-Tbps. No defense except upstream scrubbing.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only. Origin must be hidden.

security_waf.notes: Pipe dies. No on-prem device survives this volume.

ddos_appliance.notes: Even 400Gbps appliances saturate. Only ISP-level scrubbing helps.

hybrid.notes: CDN absorbs. If origin IP known, deadly.

amp_middlebox.name: Middlebox Reflection
amp_middlebox.mechanism: TCP packets to censorship middleboxes that inject HTML block pages. Amplification varies.

exposed.notes: Variable amp. Block pages can be large.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Lower volume than DNS/NTP amp.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

exposed.notes: High amp. Rare reflectors now.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

amp_wsd.name: WS-Discovery Amplification
amp_wsd.mechanism: WSD Probe to IoT devices. Amplification ~153x.

exposed.notes: IoT reflectors abundant. High volume.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturation risk.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

exposed.notes: Low amp factor. Need many reflectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Low volume. WAF handles.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial for hardware.

amp_coap.name: CoAP Amplification
amp_coap.mechanism: CoAP GET to IoT devices. Amplification ~32x.

exposed.notes: IoT reflectors growing. Moderate volume.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Moderate volume.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

exposed.notes: Moderate volume. Apple devices as reflectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN HTTP only.

security_waf.notes: Moderate volume.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

l7_http_get.name: HTTP GET Flood
l7_http_get.mechanism: High-rate HTTP GET requests with valid browser fingerprints. Each request requires full application processing (DB queries, template rendering, etc.).

exposed.notes: Origin processes every request. Hardened: connection limits, worker timeouts, caching.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN caches static. Dynamic requests pass through. Tuned: rate limiting, bot detection. Hardened: challenge pages, behavioral analysis.

security_waf.notes: DPI processes each GET. CPU collapses under volume. Tuned: rate limit rules. Hardened: DoS profiles.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection identifies GET flood patterns. Default: may pass if pattern matches legitimate. Hardened: trained baseline.

hybrid.notes: CDN absorbs cache hits. Cache misses hit appliance. Rate limiting at both layers.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall passes HTTP. Rate limiting helps.

l7_http_post.name: HTTP POST Flood
l7_http_post.mechanism: HTTP POST with valid payloads. Not cacheable - every request hits origin. Targets auth endpoints, forms, APIs.

exposed.notes: POSTs always hit origin (uncacheable). DB writes, auth checks per request.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN passes POST to origin. Default: no rate limit on POSTs. Tuned: per-endpoint rate limits.

security_waf.notes: DPI inspects POST body (SQL injection, XSS check). Massive CPU per request.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects POST flood patterns. Hardened: baseline for normal POST rate per endpoint.

hybrid.notes: CDN forwards POST. Appliance behavioral detection.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall passes HTTP POST. Rate limiting helps.

l7_http_head.name: HTTP HEAD Flood
l7_http_head.mechanism: HTTP HEAD requests. Server processes request but sends no body. Lower bandwidth but same server-side processing.

exposed.notes: Server processes full request logic for HEAD.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN can cache HEAD responses. Rate limiting.

security_waf.notes: DPI processes each HEAD.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection.

l7_http_options.name: HTTP OPTIONS Flood
l7_http_options.mechanism: HTTP OPTIONS flood. Often returns allowed methods. Some WAFs don't filter OPTIONS.

exposed.notes: Server processes OPTIONS.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN may cache or rate limit OPTIONS.

security_waf.notes: Some WAFs ignore OPTIONS (CORS preflight). May bypass rate limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection.

l7_large_download.name: Large File Download
l7_large_download.mechanism: Repeatedly download large files (PDFs, images, ZIPs). Forces server to read from disk, hold memory, and transmit. With uncompressed + cache bust, bypasses CDN and forces raw transfer.

exposed.notes: Disk I/O + bandwidth exhaustion. Each connection holds server memory for file buffer.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN caches files at edge. With cache bust: deadly (origin pull for each request). Without: CDN serves from cache.

security_waf.notes: DPI buffers entire file in memory for inspection. on-prem WAF especially vulnerable. Tuned: skip DPI for known file types.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware handles bandwidth. But with cache bust, still hits origin backend.

hybrid.notes: CDN caches. Cache bust bypasses. Appliance sees origin load.

prerequisites: Large files must exist on target (discovered by recon)
Cache bust techniques needed to bypass CDN

l7_search_flood.name: Search Query Flood
l7_search_flood.mechanism: Flood search endpoints with complex/random queries. Each query hits database with full-text search, regex, or LIKE operations.

exposed.notes: DB CPU exhaustion from complex queries. Hardened: query timeout, result limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: Search not cacheable (random queries). CDN passes to origin. Tuned: rate limit search endpoint.

security_waf.notes: WAF DPI + origin DB both exhausted. Double CPU hit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects high search rate from distributed IPs. Hardened: per-URL rate limits.

hybrid.notes: CDN can't cache search. Appliance behavioral detection.

prerequisites: Search endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

l7_api_flood.name: API Rate Abuse
l7_api_flood.mechanism: High-rate API calls. APIs typically uncacheable, each hit triggers business logic, DB queries, external service calls.

exposed.notes: API backend overwhelmed. Hardened: API gateway rate limiting.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN passes API calls through. Default: no API-specific rate limits. Tuned: per-API key rate limits.

security_waf.notes: WAF inspects API payload (JSON/XML). CPU intensive. Tuned: API-aware rate limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects API flood. Hardened: per-endpoint baselines.

prerequisites: API endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

l7_login_flood.name: Login/Auth Flood
l7_login_flood.mechanism: Flood login endpoints. Each attempt triggers auth backend (LDAP/AD query, password hash comparison, MFA check).

exposed.notes: Auth backend (LDAP, DB) overwhelmed. Hardened: account lockout, CAPTCHA.

cloud_cdn.notes: Login POST uncacheable. Default: no login rate limit. Tuned: rate limit + CAPTCHA after N failures.

security_waf.notes: WAF DPI on each login POST + auth backend load. Tuned: brute force protection.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects login flood pattern.

prerequisites: Login endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

l7_ato_credential_stuff.name: ATO Credential Stuffing
l7_ato_credential_stuff.mechanism: Replay breach-sourced username/password pairs against login endpoints, rotating source IPs (residential proxy pool) and timing to evade velocity caps. Builds on login-flood plumbing but trades volume for validity: each request attempts a REAL credential pair against a real account.

exposed.notes: No velocity controls = full compromise. Hardened: account lockout + MFA.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN bot-management sees suspicious login pattern. Hardened: behavioural scoring + device fingerprint + per-user velocity.

security_waf.notes: WAF can flag repeated failed logins per source, but residential rotation defeats per-IP rules.

ddos_appliance.notes: DDoS appliances focus on volume, not credential correlation. Most pass through.

hybrid.notes: Only the CDN layer has bot-management context; appliance does not help.

prerequisites: Breach credentials available for this domain (HIBP/DeHashed/LeakCheck)
Sanctioned test accounts for tier 2+ execution

l7_ato_password_spray.name: ATO Password Spray
l7_ato_password_spray.mechanism: Low-rate spray: ONE common password (e.g. Winter2026!) across MANY usernames, slow enough to stay below per-account velocity thresholds. Opposite of credential stuffing: volume of users, not volume of passwords.

exposed.notes: No per-user or per-IP rate limit = compromise a % of accounts with weak passwords.

cloud_cdn.notes: Bot-management may flag the pattern; spray typically below per-IP thresholds. Hardened: behavioural correlation across source IPs.

security_waf.notes: WAF typically measures per-IP, not per-password-across-users.

ddos_appliance.notes: Out of scope - spray is not volumetric.

hybrid.notes: Only cloud bot-management has cross-IP correlation context.

prerequisites: Username enumeration feasible (directory harvest, breach dumps, LinkedIn scrape)

l7_graphql_complex.name: GraphQL Complexity
l7_graphql_complex.mechanism: Deeply nested GraphQL queries that cause exponential server-side processing. Single request can trigger millions of DB joins.

exposed.notes: Exponential complexity. Single query can down a server. Hardened: query depth/cost limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN passes GraphQL POST. Default: no query analysis. Tuned: depth limits at CDN WAF level.

security_waf.notes: WAF DPI on GraphQL body + origin processes complex query. Few WAFs understand GraphQL nesting.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance doesn't understand GraphQL. Passes through. Hardened: response time anomaly detection.

hybrid.notes: Neither CDN nor appliance understands GraphQL complexity by default.

prerequisites: GraphQL endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

l7_http_bomb.name: HTTP Decompression Bomb
l7_http_bomb.mechanism: Send gzip-compressed POST payload that expands to enormous size. Server decompresses in memory.

exposed.notes: Server decompresses gzip. Memory exhaustion. Hardened: decompress limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN may decompress or pass through. Tuned: body size limits.

security_waf.notes: WAF decompresses for DPI. Memory bomb hits WAF before origin. Very effective against DPI.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance may not decompress. Passes through.

prerequisites: Target must accept gzip Content-Encoding on POST

l7_random_path.name: Random Path Flood
l7_random_path.mechanism: GET requests to random paths (/abc123, /xyz789). Forces application routing, 404 processing, logging. Uncacheable.

exposed.notes: Application processes each path. 404 handlers often expensive (DB lookup, template render).

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN can cache 404s (default: often not). Tuned: cache 404, rate limit high-404-rate IPs.

security_waf.notes: DPI processes each request. Random paths avoid URL-specific rules.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects high 404 rate.

l7_cache_bust.name: Cache Bust Attack
l7_cache_bust.mechanism: Append random query parameters (?_=random) to force CDN cache MISS. Every request hits origin. CDN becomes expensive pass-through.

exposed.notes: No CDN to bust, but random params still bypass application-level caching.

cloud_cdn.notes: DEFAULT: CDN caches by full URL including query string. Random params = 100% miss rate. Tuned: ignore query params for caching. Hardened: sort/normalize query params.

security_waf.notes: WAF doesn't cache. All requests hit origin regardless.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance doesn't cache. But behavioral detection of high unique-URL rate.

hybrid.notes: Cache bust bypasses CDN layer. All requests hit appliance and origin.

prerequisites: CDN must cache by query string (most do by default)
Technique: append random ?_=timestamp to each request

l7_redos.name: ReDoS
l7_redos.mechanism: Craft input that triggers catastrophic backtracking in server-side regex. Single request can consume 100% CPU for seconds.

exposed.notes: Application regex processes input. Hardened: regex timeout, RE2 engine.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN passes to origin. Default: no regex analysis. Tuned: rate limit POST endpoints.

security_waf.notes: WAF's OWN regex rules may be vulnerable to ReDoS. Ironic: the WAF can be the victim.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance passes through. Origin processes regex. Hardened: response time anomaly.

prerequisites: Vulnerable regex pattern on target (discovered by fuzzing)

l7_http2_rapid_reset.name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset (CVE-2023-44487)
l7_http2_rapid_reset.mechanism: Open HTTP/2 stream and immediately RST_STREAM. Server allocates resources for stream but client cancels before response. Thousands of streams per connection.

exposed.notes: Unpatched servers extremely vulnerable. Hardened: patched + stream concurrency limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: Major CDNs patched Oct 2023. CDN terminates HTTP/2 and re-multiplexes to origin.

security_waf.notes: WAF HTTP/2 stack may be vulnerable itself. Few WAFs properly track rapid stream resets.

ddos_appliance.notes: Newer firmware detects rapid reset pattern. Older: vulnerable.

l7_http2_continuation.name: HTTP/2 CONTINUATION Flood (CVE-2024-27316)
l7_http2_continuation.mechanism: Send HEADERS frame without END_HEADERS flag, followed by many CONTINUATION frames. Server must buffer all headers before processing.

exposed.notes: Unpatched Apache/nginx/Go extremely vulnerable. Memory exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates HTTP/2. Major CDNs patched early 2024.

security_waf.notes: WAF HTTP/2 parser may be vulnerable itself. Memory exhaustion from header buffering.

ddos_appliance.notes: Newer firmware limits CONTINUATION frames.

l7_http2_ping.name: HTTP/2 PING Flood
l7_http2_ping.mechanism: Flood HTTP/2 PING frames. Server must respond to each PING immediately. Disrupts frame scheduling.

exposed.notes: Server CPU on PING responses. Hardened: PING rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN limits PING rate per connection.

security_waf.notes: WAF handles PING at TCP level.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

l7_http2_settings.name: HTTP/2 SETTINGS Flood
l7_http2_settings.mechanism: Flood SETTINGS frames requiring ACK. Each requires processing and ACK response.

exposed.notes: CPU on SETTINGS processing.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN limits SETTINGS rate.

security_waf.notes: WAF processes SETTINGS.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

l7_http2_empty.name: HTTP/2 Empty Frame Flood
l7_http2_empty.mechanism: Flood empty DATA frames on open streams. Forces frame processing without payload.

exposed.notes: Frame processing overhead. Lower impact than other H2 vectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops empty frames.

security_waf.notes: Frame processing.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

l7_quic_initial.name: QUIC Initial Flood
l7_quic_initial.mechanism: Flood QUIC Initial packets. Server performs TLS 1.3 handshake + crypto for each. High CPU cost per packet.

exposed.notes: Crypto handshake per Initial. CPU exhaustion. Hardened: retry token, address validation.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN has massive QUIC capacity + rate limiting.

security_waf.notes: WAF cannot inspect QUIC (encrypted). Passes through.

ddos_appliance.notes: Newer appliances: QUIC-aware rate limiting. Older: pass through.

l7_quic_cid.name: QUIC Connection ID Exhaustion
l7_quic_cid.mechanism: Exhaust QUIC connection ID space. Each CID requires server state.

exposed.notes: State table exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN manages CID at scale.

security_waf.notes: WAF doesn't manage QUIC CIDs.

ddos_appliance.notes: CID tracking if QUIC-aware.

l7_quic_version.name: QUIC Version Negotiation Abuse
l7_quic_version.mechanism: Send unknown QUIC version. Server responds with Version Negotiation packet. Amplification from server responses.

exposed.notes: Server CPU on version negotiation. Low amp factor.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN handles.

security_waf.notes: Passes through.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

l7_quic_ack.name: QUIC ACK Manipulation
l7_quic_ack.mechanism: Send manipulated ACK frames to confuse congestion control. Can cause server to retransmit or stall.

exposed.notes: Congestion control confusion. Service degradation.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN handles.

security_waf.notes: Passes through.

ddos_appliance.notes: QUIC awareness.

l7_quic_malformed.name: QUIC Malformed Frame Flood
l7_quic_malformed.mechanism: Flood malformed QUIC frames. Server must parse and error-handle each.

exposed.notes: Error handling overhead.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops malformed.

security_waf.notes: Passes through.

ddos_appliance.notes: Drops malformed.

l7_quic_0rtt.name: QUIC 0-RTT Replay
l7_quic_0rtt.mechanism: Replay 0-RTT data from captured handshake. Server processes request before authentication completes. Amplification without own crypto cost.

exposed.notes: 0-RTT replay skips handshake. Server processes captured requests. Hardened: 0-RTT disabled or limited.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN limits 0-RTT replay.

security_waf.notes: WAF can't distinguish replay from legitimate 0-RTT.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection of replayed requests.

l7_http3_headers.name: HTTP/3 HEADERS Flood
l7_http3_headers.mechanism: Flood HTTP/3 HEADERS frames over QUIC. Each requires QPACK decompression + request processing.

exposed.notes: QPACK decompression + request processing. Newer attack surface.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates HTTP/3.

security_waf.notes: WAF doesn't inspect HTTP/3. Passes through.

ddos_appliance.notes: Limited HTTP/3 awareness.

l7_quic_reflect.name: QUIC Reflection
l7_quic_reflect.mechanism: Spoofed QUIC Initial to servers, causing Retry or Version Negotiation responses sent to victim. Low amp factor (~3-5x).

exposed.notes: Low amplification. Needs many reflectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN drops unsolicited QUIC responses.

security_waf.notes: Low volume.

ddos_appliance.notes: Drops.

slow_slowloris.name: Slowloris
slow_slowloris.mechanism: Open HTTP connections and send headers very slowly (one byte/sec). Never completes request. Holds connection slots indefinitely.

exposed.notes: Apache/nginx connection pool exhausted. 10K connections = server down. Hardened: aggressive header timeout (5s).

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates slow connections with strict timeouts (typically 30s header timeout).

security_waf.notes: WAF connection table shared with IPS/AV. Drains much faster than rated. Tuned: header timeout.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects connections with incomplete headers. Large connection table (40M+). Hardened: idle connection cleanup.

slow_slow_post.name: Slow POST
slow_slow_post.mechanism: Send POST with large Content-Length, then drip body byte-by-byte. Server holds thread/worker waiting for complete body.

exposed.notes: Worker threads blocked waiting for body. Hardened: body receive timeout.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN body receive timeout (typically 60s). Buffers before forwarding to origin.

security_waf.notes: WAF buffers POST body for DPI. Memory + connection held. Shared resources drain.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects slow body rate. Connection timeout.

slow_rudy.name: R.U.D.Y.
slow_rudy.mechanism: POST to forms with Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded, send one form field byte at a time. Apache specifically vulnerable.

exposed.notes: Apache mod_cgi holds thread per connection. Same as Slow POST but form-specific.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN timeout.

security_waf.notes: Same as Slow POST.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral detection.

slow_slow_read.name: Slow Read
slow_slow_read.mechanism: Request large response, then read at 10 bytes/sec (tiny TCP window). Server holds send buffer and connection open.

exposed.notes: Server buffers response in memory. Combined with LFD = massive memory hold.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN may buffer response. But CDN-to-client connection still held. Tuned: client send timeout.

security_waf.notes: WAF buffers response for DPI. Memory exhaustion. Shared with IPS/AV.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects zero/tiny window connections. Send timeout.

prerequisites: Large response available (LFD target from recon)

slow_tls_renegotiation.name: TLS Renegotiation
slow_tls_renegotiation.mechanism: Repeatedly initiate TLS renegotiation on established connections. Server does ~15x more crypto work than client.

exposed.notes: CPU exhaustion from repeated key exchange. Hardened: disable renegotiation (TLS 1.3 has no renegotiation).

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN terminates TLS. Renegotiation limited or disabled.

security_waf.notes: WAF SSL offload CPU. Renegotiation multiplies crypto cost. Tuned: limit renegotiation count.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware SSL offload. Rate limit renegotiation. TLS 1.3: no renegotiation.

prerequisites: Target must support TLS 1.2 or older (TLS 1.3 removed renegotiation)

slow_tls_reconnection.name: TLS Reconnection Flood
slow_tls_reconnection.mechanism: Rapidly connect/disconnect TLS. Each handshake costs server CPU (ECDHE key exchange). Client reuses session ticket, server may not.

exposed.notes: Each new handshake = ECDHE computation. Hardened: session resumption, handshake rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN has massive TLS capacity.

security_waf.notes: WAF SSL offload CPU. Each handshake expensive.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware SSL acceleration. Rate limit new TLS handshakes.

slow_http2_window.name: HTTP/2 Slow Read (Window Exhaustion)
slow_http2_window.mechanism: Set HTTP/2 flow control window to 0 (or very small). Server buffers response data. Memory exhaustion similar to TCP slow read but at HTTP/2 stream level.

exposed.notes: Server buffers per-stream. Many streams x large responses = memory exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN may enforce stream window minimums. Tuned: stream timeout.

security_waf.notes: WAF HTTP/2 buffer memory shared with DPI engine.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects zero-window streams. Timeout.

slow_empty_connection.name: Empty TCP Connection Flood
slow_empty_connection.mechanism: Complete TCP handshake, keep connection open, never send data. Exhaust server connection slots without triggering request-level monitoring.

exposed.notes: Connection table exhaustion. Hardened: aggressive idle timeout (10s).

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN idle timeout kills empty connections.

security_waf.notes: WAF connection table shared. Idle connections still consume slots.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects connections with no data. Large table helps.

prerequisites: Requires real TCP connections from bot IPs

sip_invite.name: SIP INVITE Flood
sip_invite.mechanism: Flood SIP INVITE to VoIP servers. Each INVITE triggers call setup processing.

exposed.notes: SIP server processes each INVITE (session creation, auth, codec negotiation).

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: WAF may inspect SIP if configured. Most don't.

ddos_appliance.notes: SIP-aware appliances rate limit INVITE.

prerequisites: Target must run SIP server (UDP/5060 or TCP/5060)

sip_bye.name: SIP BYE Flood
sip_bye.mechanism: Spoofed BYE to tear down active calls.

exposed.notes: Active calls terminated. Hardened: strict session validation.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: WAF may inspect.

ddos_appliance.notes: Session validation.

sip_register.name: SIP REGISTER Flood
sip_register.mechanism: Flood REGISTER to exhaust registrar database and auth processing.

exposed.notes: Registrar DB overwhelmed. Auth processing per REGISTER.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: WAF may inspect.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit REGISTER.

sip_options.name: SIP OPTIONS Flood
sip_options.mechanism: Flood SIP OPTIONS. Low overhead but still requires server processing.

exposed.notes: Lightweight. Mainly useful for reconnaissance/scanning.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: Low overhead.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

sip_cancel.name: SIP CANCEL Flood
sip_cancel.mechanism: Cancel pending INVITEs to waste processing.

exposed.notes: Cancel processing.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: Low overhead.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

sip_reflect.name: SIP Amplification
sip_reflect.mechanism: Spoofed SIP OPTIONS to open SIP servers. Amplification ~10x.

exposed.notes: Volume from many reflectors.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: Lower amp factor.

ddos_appliance.notes: Drops.

sip_malformed.name: SIP Malformed Request
sip_malformed.mechanism: Malformed SIP messages to crash parser or trigger error handling.

exposed.notes: Parser error handling.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SIP.

security_waf.notes: WAF drops malformed.

ddos_appliance.notes: Drops malformed.

smtp_rcpt.name: SMTP RCPT TO Flood
smtp_rcpt.mechanism: Flood RCPT TO to mail server. Each recipient requires address validation (LDAP/DB lookup).

exposed.notes: LDAP/DB lookup per recipient. Hardened: recipient rate limit, greylisting.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: Firewall rate limits SMTP connections.

ddos_appliance.notes: SMTP-aware rate limiting.

smtp_ehlo.name: SMTP EHLO Flood
smtp_ehlo.mechanism: Rapid SMTP connection + EHLO to exhaust connection slots.

exposed.notes: Connection slot exhaustion. Lower impact than RCPT flood.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit connections.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial to detect.

smtp_data.name: SMTP DATA Flood
smtp_data.mechanism: Send large email messages. Server stores to disk, runs content filter, AV scan.

exposed.notes: Disk I/O + content filter CPU. Hardened: message size limit, rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: Content filter CPU.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit messages.

smtp_auth.name: SMTP AUTH Flood
smtp_auth.mechanism: Flood SMTP AUTH. Each attempt triggers auth backend (LDAP/AD).

exposed.notes: Auth backend exhaustion. Hardened: auth rate limit, fail2ban.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit auth.

ddos_appliance.notes: Auth rate limit.

prerequisites: Target must run SMTP server with auth

smtp_bounce.name: SMTP Bounce Flood
smtp_bounce.mechanism: Send mail to non-existent recipients. Server generates NDR (bounce) messages to spoofed sender.

exposed.notes: NDR generation CPU + disk. Hardened: disable NDR for unknown senders.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

smtp_backscatter.name: SMTP Backscatter
smtp_backscatter.mechanism: Spoofed sender=victim, send to servers that generate bounces TO the victim. Indirect flood.

exposed.notes: Bounce messages flood victim's MX. Lower volume than direct.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SMTP.

security_waf.notes: SMTP rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial volume.

ssh_handshake.name: SSH Handshake Flood
ssh_handshake.mechanism: Rapid SSH connection initiation. Each handshake requires DH key exchange (CPU intensive).

exposed.notes: DH key exchange CPU. Hardened: MaxStartups, fail2ban.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SSH.

security_waf.notes: Firewall rate limits SSH.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

ssh_auth.name: SSH Auth Flood
ssh_auth.mechanism: Brute force SSH authentication. Each attempt triggers PAM/LDAP auth.

exposed.notes: Auth backend exhaustion. Hardened: key-only auth, fail2ban.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy SSH.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

prerequisites: SSH must be exposed with password auth enabled

rdp_flood.name: RDP Connection Flood
rdp_flood.mechanism: Flood RDP connection initiation. TLS + CredSSP handshake CPU cost.

exposed.notes: RDP handshake CPU. Hardened: NLA, rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy RDP.

security_waf.notes: Firewall rate limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

rdp_reflect.name: RDP Amplification

exposed.notes: High amp. Volume.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy RDP.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturation risk.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops RDP UDP.

prerequisites: Exposed RDP servers with UDP enabled

telnet_flood.name: Telnet Flood
telnet_flood.mechanism: Flood telnet connection attempts. Low resource cost to attacker.

exposed.notes: Connection exhaustion. Hardened: disable telnet.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy telnet.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

ldap_query.name: LDAP Query Flood
ldap_query.mechanism: Flood complex LDAP queries. Each requires directory tree traversal.

exposed.notes: Directory CPU from complex queries. Hardened: query complexity limits.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy LDAP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit LDAP.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

cldap_reflect.name: CLDAP Amplification
cldap_reflect.mechanism: Connectionless LDAP amplification ~56-70x. Duplicate of amp_cldap for protocol category.

exposed.notes: High amp. See amp_cldap.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy.

security_waf.notes: Pipe saturates.

ddos_appliance.notes: Hardware drops.

ldap_bind.name: LDAP Bind Flood
ldap_bind.mechanism: Flood LDAP bind (auth) requests. Each triggers password validation.

exposed.notes: Auth processing per bind. Hardened: rate limit, account lockout.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy LDAP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

mqtt_connect.name: MQTT CONNECT Flood
mqtt_connect.mechanism: Flood MQTT CONNECT to broker. Each connection requires auth + session state.

exposed.notes: Broker connection table exhaustion. Hardened: max connections, auth rate limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy MQTT.

security_waf.notes: Firewall rate limits.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

mqtt_publish.name: MQTT PUBLISH Flood
mqtt_publish.mechanism: Flood MQTT PUBLISH messages. Broker must route to all subscribers.

exposed.notes: Broker CPU + memory for message routing. Hardened: message rate limit, ACL.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy MQTT.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

prerequisites: Must authenticate or broker allows anonymous

coap_flood.name: CoAP Request Flood
coap_flood.mechanism: Flood CoAP requests to IoT devices. UDP-based, each requires processing.

exposed.notes: IoT device overwhelmed. Limited CPU/memory.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy CoAP.

security_waf.notes: Low volume needed.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

modbus_read.name: Modbus Read Flood
modbus_read.mechanism: Flood Modbus read requests to PLCs/RTUs. Industrial devices have minimal CPU.

exposed.notes: PLC CPU exhausted. Industrial devices not designed for high request rates.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy Modbus.

security_waf.notes: Industrial WAF if present.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

modbus_write.name: Modbus Write Flood
modbus_write.mechanism: Flood Modbus write coils/registers. Can cause physical process disruption.

exposed.notes: Physical process disruption + CPU exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy Modbus.

security_waf.notes: Industrial WAF.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

dnp3_flood.name: DNP3 Flood
dnp3_flood.mechanism: Flood DNP3 requests to SCADA systems.

exposed.notes: SCADA CPU exhausted.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy DNP3.

security_waf.notes: Industrial WAF.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

rtsp_describe.name: RTSP DESCRIBE Flood
rtsp_describe.mechanism: Flood RTSP DESCRIBE to streaming servers. Each request requires session setup processing.

exposed.notes: Streaming server session processing.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy RTSP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

rtsp_setup.name: RTSP SETUP Flood
rtsp_setup.mechanism: RTSP SETUP to allocate media transport channels. Server allocates RTP ports per session.

exposed.notes: Port allocation + session state exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy RTSP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

rtmp_flood.name: RTMP Connect Flood
rtmp_flood.mechanism: Flood RTMP connect + handshake to streaming servers.

exposed.notes: Handshake CPU + connection slots.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy RTMP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

hls_segment.name: HLS Segment Flood
hls_segment.mechanism: Flood HLS .ts segment requests. Each segment is a video chunk (2-10s of video). High bandwidth per request.

exposed.notes: Bandwidth exhaustion from large video segments. Disk I/O.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN caches HLS segments at edge. Very effective caching.

security_waf.notes: DPI on video segments = massive CPU + memory.

ddos_appliance.notes: Bandwidth. Behavioral detection.

prerequisites: HLS endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

xmpp_stanza.name: XMPP Stanza Flood
xmpp_stanza.mechanism: Flood XMPP message stanzas. Server must route, store, and possibly archive each.

exposed.notes: Message routing + storage CPU.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy XMPP.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Rate limit.

xmpp_presence.name: XMPP Presence Flood
xmpp_presence.mechanism: Rapid presence status changes. Server must broadcast to all contacts.

exposed.notes: Broadcast CPU. Lower impact than stanza flood.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

xmpp_iq.name: XMPP IQ Flood
xmpp_iq.mechanism: Flood XMPP info/query stanzas. Server must process each query.

exposed.notes: Query processing.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN doesn't proxy.

security_waf.notes: Rate limit.

ddos_appliance.notes: Trivial.

ws_flood.name: WebSocket Connection Flood
ws_flood.mechanism: Flood WebSocket upgrade requests. Each connection holds server resources for persistent bidirectional channel.

exposed.notes: WebSocket connections are persistent - server holds state per connection. Pool exhaustion.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN may proxy WebSocket. Default: limited WS connections. Tuned: rate limit WS upgrades.

security_waf.notes: WAF processes WS upgrade. Persistent connections drain connection table.

ddos_appliance.notes: Behavioral: detects rapid WS upgrades. Connection limit.

prerequisites: WebSocket endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

ws_message.name: WebSocket Message Flood
ws_message.mechanism: Send rapid messages on established WebSocket connections. Server processes each message (JSON parse, business logic, broadcast).

exposed.notes: Server processes each message. Broadcast to other clients amplifies.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN proxies WS. Tuned: message rate limit.

security_waf.notes: WAF may inspect WS messages. CPU per message.

ddos_appliance.notes: Message rate detection.

grpc_stream.name: gRPC Stream Flood
grpc_stream.mechanism: Flood gRPC streaming RPCs. Each stream holds server resources. Protobuf deserialization per message.

exposed.notes: gRPC server processes each stream. Protobuf deserialization CPU.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN gRPC proxy support varies. cloud CDN/WAF supports gRPC.

security_waf.notes: WAFs generally don't understand gRPC/protobuf. Pass through.

ddos_appliance.notes: Limited gRPC awareness.

prerequisites: gRPC endpoint must exist (discovered by recon)

grpc_metadata.name: gRPC Metadata Flood
grpc_metadata.mechanism: Flood gRPC calls with oversized metadata headers. Server must parse and validate.

exposed.notes: Metadata parsing + validation CPU. Hardened: metadata size limit.

cloud_cdn.notes: CDN header size limits.

security_waf.notes: WAF processes headers.

ddos_appliance.notes: Header size limits.

direct_origin_attack.name: Direct Origin Attack (CDN Bypass)
direct_origin_attack.mechanism: Discover origin IP (DNS history, MX records, SPF, cert transparency, subdomain enum) and attack directly, bypassing all CDN/WAF protection.

exposed.notes: Already exposed - this IS the default state.

cloud_cdn.notes: If origin IP found: all CDN protection bypassed. Hardened: origin lockdown (only accept CDN IPs).

security_waf.notes: On-prem WAF IS the target. No bypass needed.

security_waf.notes: WAF doesn't understand app logic. Passes through to origin.

ddos_appliance.notes: Appliance doesn't inspect app logic deeply. Behavioral detection of anomalies.

hybrid.notes: CDN rate limits. Appliance behavioral detection. App logic still complex.

perimeter_filtered.notes: Firewall passes HTTP. No app-logic inspection.

_modifiers_note: Technique modifiers (cache-bust, range abuse, image resize, pagination abuse, etc.) and strategy modifiers (browser fingerprint rotation, script-kiddie posture, etc.) compose with the base effectiveness ratings to predict how attack variants land against each architecture. The composition rule and per-modifier shifts are not part of the public release. Contact DDactic for the full framework.

key_insights: Cloud CDN blocks ALL non-HTTP protocols (L3, L4, SIP, SMTP, SSH, etc.) by design
Security WAFs (F5, Check Point, FortiGate) are MOST vulnerable to L7 floods and slow attacks because DPI shares CPU with IPS/AV/SSL
DDoS appliances (Radware DefensePro, Arbor) are hardware-accelerated but still vulnerable to pipe saturation
Cache bust is the key technique against CDN - turns CDN into expensive pass-through
GraphQL complexity and ReDoS can be deadly against ANY architecture because they exploit application logic, not network capacity
QUIC/HTTP3 vectors bypass most WAFs and older DDoS appliances that don't support QUIC inspection
Direct origin attack (CDN bypass) is the most impactful vector against cloud-protected assets
Protocol-specific vectors (SIP, SMTP, MQTT, Modbus) only apply to assets running those services
Amplification vectors (NTP, DNS, Memcached) require spoofed source IPs - only effective against non-CDN assets
Config level matters most for L7 attacks - behavioral baselines take 2+ weeks to train
